dailyUpdates.label: Federal
dailyUpdates.headline: State Condemns Federal Deferral of IHSS Funds, Warns of Immediate Harm to Hundreds of Thousands of Vulnerable Californians
dailyUpdates.summary: California is pushing back against a federal decision to defer In-Home Supportive Services (IHSS) funding, warning that the delay will cause immediate harm to hundreds of thousands of vulnerable Medi-Cal beneficiaries. For ECM and Community Supports providers, IHSS funding disruptions could destabilize a key long-term services and supports program that intersects with CalAIM's high-need populations.

dailyUpdates.label: Waiver
dailyUpdates.headline: California Releases First Impact Report on Groundbreaking Justice-Involved Reentry Initiative
dailyUpdates.summary: DHCS has released its first impact report on the CalAIM Justice-Involved (JI) Reentry Initiative, which provides Medi-Cal services to individuals 90 days before release from incarceration under the 1115 waiver. This is directly relevant to ECM and Community Supports providers serving the justice-involved population of focus, offering early data on program reach and outcomes.

dailyUpdates.label: Federal
dailyUpdates.headline: Looming Medicaid Cuts Supercharge California's Latest Labor-Industry Fight
dailyUpdates.summary: Anticipated federal Medicaid cuts under H.R. 1 are intensifying a California labor-industry battle, with SEIU-UHW sponsoring ballot initiatives to regulate community clinics and cap executive pay at hospitals and physician groups. Potential Medi-Cal funding reductions could significantly affect the provider ecosystem that delivers ECM and Community Supports services to high-need beneficiaries.

dailyUpdates.label: Medi-Cal Managed Care
dailyUpdates.headline: Attachment A: Network Provider Agreement Template Checklist
dailyUpdates.summary: DHCS has posted an updated Network Provider Agreement Template Checklist, which governs contractual requirements between Medi-Cal managed care plans and their network providers. ECM and Community Supports providers contracting with MCPs should review this checklist to ensure their provider agreements meet current DHCS standards.

dailyUpdates.label: Medi-Cal Managed Care
dailyUpdates.headline: Quality Measures for Encounter Data — Version 2.0
dailyUpdates.summary: DHCS has released Version 2.0 of its Quality Measures for Encounter Data guidance, updating reporting requirements for Medi-Cal managed care plans. Accurate encounter data submission is critical for ECM and Community Supports providers, as it underpins MCP oversight, rate-setting, and program evaluation under CalAIM.

dailyUpdates.label: Federal
dailyUpdates.headline: State Condemns Federal Deferral of IHSS Funds, Warns of Immediate Harm to Hundreds of Thousands of Vulnerable Californians
dailyUpdates.summary: California is pushing back against a federal decision to defer In-Home Supportive Services (IHSS) funding, warning that the action threatens immediate harm to hundreds of thousands of vulnerable Medi-Cal beneficiaries. For ECM and Community Supports providers, disruptions to IHSS funding could destabilize a key long-term services and supports (LTSS) program that intersects directly with CalAIM's high-need populations.

dailyUpdates.label: Medi-Cal Managed Care
dailyUpdates.headline: Attachment A: Network Provider Agreement Template Checklist
dailyUpdates.summary: DHCS has published an Attachment A Network Provider Agreement Template Checklist, which is a managed care contracting tool relevant to Medi-Cal Managed Care Plan (MCP) network adequacy requirements. ECM and Community Supports providers should monitor this checklist as it may affect how MCPs structure provider agreements and network compliance documentation.

dailyUpdates.label: Medi-Cal Managed Care
dailyUpdates.headline: Quality Measures for Encounter Data — Version 2.0
dailyUpdates.summary: DHCS has released Version 2.0 of the Quality Measures for Encounter Data, updating the framework used to assess Medi-Cal managed care plan performance based on encounter data submissions. ECM and Community Supports providers should be aware that encounter data quality directly affects MCP reporting obligations and may influence program compliance and payment reconciliation.

dailyUpdates.label: Waiver / Justice-Involved
dailyUpdates.headline: California Releases First Impact Report on Groundbreaking Justice-Involved Reentry Initiative
dailyUpdates.summary: DHCS has published the first impact report on California's Justice-Involved Reentry Initiative, a flagship CalAIM component that provides Medi-Cal coverage and care coordination to people leaving incarceration. This report is directly relevant to ECM providers serving the justice-involved population of focus, offering early outcome data that may shape future program guidance and funding decisions.

news.label: Federal
news.headline: State Condemns Federal Deferral of IHSS Funds, Warns of Immediate Harm to Hundreds of Thousands of Vulnerable Californians
news.summary: California's state government has formally condemned a federal decision to defer In-Home Supportive Services (IHSS) funding, warning of immediate harm to hundreds of thousands of vulnerable Californians who rely on the program. IHSS is a key Community Supports-adjacent service for Medi-Cal beneficiaries, and funding disruptions could destabilize care for populations that CalAIM's Enhanced Care Management program also serves.

news.label: Federal
news.headline: Millions of Kids Could Lose Insurance as GOP Healthcare Cuts Start To Bite
news.summary: Federal cuts embedded in the 2025 GOP budget law (H.R. 1) are beginning to affect Medicaid coverage, with millions of children at risk of losing insurance as states face new eligibility and financing constraints. California's Medi-Cal program, including CalAIM's managed care and ECM infrastructure, could face enrollment and funding pressures as these cuts take effect.

news.label: Waiver / Reentry
news.headline: California Releases First Impact Report on Groundbreaking Justice-Involved Reentry Initiative
news.summary: DHCS published its first impact report on California's justice-involved reentry initiative, a CalAIM flagship program that extends Medi-Cal services — including ECM and Community Supports — to incarcerated individuals 90 days prior to release. The report marks a significant milestone in evaluating the program's outcomes and informing future 1115 waiver negotiations with the federal government.

policy.label: Managed Care
policy.headline: Attachment A: Network Provider Agreement Template Checklist
policy.summary: DHCS released an updated Network Provider Agreement Template Checklist, which governs contracting standards between Medi-Cal managed care plans and their network providers. This document is directly relevant to managed care network adequacy requirements under CalAIM and associated APL compliance obligations.

policy.label: Managed Care / Quality
policy.headline: Quality Measures for Encounter Data — Version 2.0
policy.summary: DHCS published Version 2.0 of its Quality Measures for Encounter Data, updating the technical specifications used to assess Medi-Cal managed care plan performance through encounter data submissions. Accurate encounter data is foundational to CalAIM's value-based care goals and DHCS oversight of plan quality.

policy.label: Managed Care / Behavioral Health
policy.headline: Attachment A: DHCS-Approved Youth Trauma Screening Tools for Specialty Mental Health Services Access Criteria (APL 26-002)
policy.summary: DHCS released Attachment A to APL 26-002, providing the official list of approved youth trauma screening tools that managed care plans and county mental health plans must use when determining access to Specialty Mental Health Services. This guidance is relevant to CalAIM's behavioral health integration efforts and the screening requirements tied to ECM and broader Medi-Cal managed care contracts.

news.label: Federal
news.headline: How Massive Federal Cuts Will Create Unprecedented Challenges for Medi-Cal Patients and Providers
news.summary: CHCF analysis of H.R. 1: up to 2M Californians losing coverage, $30B/year in lost federal funds, and cascading effects on providers, plans, and safety-net infrastructure.

news.label: Federal
news.headline: H.R. 1 Cuts to Medi-Cal Impact Californians in Every Legislative District
news.summary: Cal Budget Center maps H.R. 1 Medi-Cal cuts by legislative district, showing broad geographic reach of eligibility losses and reduced federal matching funds.

news.label: Legislative
news.headline: Will California Restore Medi-Cal for Undocumented Immigrants?
news.summary: CalMatters covers the push behind SB 1422 and the political dynamics of restoring Medi-Cal enrollment for undocumented adults amid the state's budget crunch and federal pressure.

news.label: Legislative
news.headline: California Lawmakers Introduce Bills to Lessen Harmful Impact of H.R. 1 for Medi-Cal Enrollees
news.summary: NHeLP covers AB 2161 and companion bills designed to minimize administrative burden of H.R. 1 work requirements on Medi-Cal members, including exemption protections.

news.label: Waiver
news.headline: National Context for California's Renewal of CalAIM in 2026
news.summary: CHCS situates CalAIM's waiver renewal within the national Medicaid landscape, noting CMS restrictions on ILOS signal a tougher negotiating environment for the 2027-2031 renewal.

news.label: ECM
news.headline: The Future of Enhanced Care Management: Why CalAIM Must Be Protected
news.summary: Provider-perspective advocacy piece making the case for protecting ECM in the waiver renewal, citing utilization data and risk of rollback under federal budget pressure.

news.label: Budget
news.headline: The 2026-27 Budget: Medi-Cal Fiscal Outlook
news.summary: LAO highlights the combined squeeze of H.R. 1 cuts and state budget constraints, including proposed ECM spending of $956M and CS spending of $231M for 2025-26.

policy.label: Waiver
policy.headline: CalAIM 1915(b) Waiver Renewal Comment Period Open
policy.summary: DHCS opened a 30-day public comment period (May 21 to June 20, 2026) on the five-year CalAIM 1915(b) renewal covering managed care, SMHS, dental, and DMC-ODS. Proposed renewal runs 2027-2031.

policy.label: Federal
policy.headline: H.R. 1 Cuts ~$30B/Year from Medi-Cal — Implementation Underway
policy.summary: Key CA impacts: retroactive coverage reduced to 2 months (2027), some immigrants lose eligibility Oct 2026, and 80hrs/month work requirements take effect Jan 2027.

policy.label: Managed Care
policy.headline: New APLs: BH Data-Sharing (26-004) and MH Referral Coordination (26-002)
policy.summary: APL 26-002 updates the MH referral coordination framework between MCPs and county mental health plans. APL 26-004 covers BH data-sharing — both directly affect ECM behavioral health workflows.

policy.label: Community Supports
policy.headline: CS Reaches 520K+ Members — 1.3M Services Delivered Statewide
policy.summary: 94% of Medi-Cal members now have access to the full suite of 15 CS services. Housing-related supports including the new Transitional Rent service are broadly available statewide.

policy.label: ECM
policy.headline: LAO Flags ECM Reach Gap — Only 20% of Homeless Members Enrolled
policy.summary: A March 2025 LAO report found ECM utilization at just 0.9% of MCP members in 2024, with only about 20% of homeless-identified members receiving ECM — flagged ahead of the waiver renewal.

policy.label: Legislative
policy.headline: AB 2161 and SB 1422 — Work Requirements and Immigrant Coverage
policy.summary: AB 2161 shapes how CA implements H.R. 1 work requirements to minimize member burden. SB 1422 would restore full-scope Medi-Cal for immigrant adults 19+. SB 1422 ordered to third reading.

policy.label: Managed Care
policy.headline: Medi-Medi Plans Open for Enrollment in Most Counties (2026)
policy.summary: Dual-eligible Medicare and Medi-Cal Medi-Medi Plans launched enrollment across most California counties in 2026 as part of managed care restructuring under CalAIM.
